channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Lied my ass off, got a six figure job plz help. — 39 replies — 65.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Lied my ass off, got a six figure job plz help.

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-04-20 18:56 UTC

OP

okay anons, I'll be blunt as fuck.

Flat-out lied on my resume. Econ degree, data analyst gig at some regional firm. Only good thing was tons of free time so I grinded leetcode non-stop cuz I had nothing better to do.

made a fake resume, somehow snagged six-figure mid-level SWE job. 20 days pto, free food, 4 weeks work from anywhere.

Start date 4 weeks away. Can I just use Claude or Copilot to carry me? I have never done any shipping of actual code ever. never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews. i just grinded python and leetcode 24/7.

please respond I'm scared as fuck. Am I cooked or does this actually work? Anything I should learn before even starting or should I just give up and not show up? Anyone pull this off?

Should i aim for product management instead?

Early replies (first 3)

62130795
remain confident and hedge your bets by applying elsewhere. do not think of yourself as in trouble, you are just a LARPing spy seeing how far you can make it to infiltrate their systems. people perform better when they think like this. just try to fit in, figure things out but be ready to jump ship when it's time. rinse and repeat and eventually you're legit

62130812
>>62130783
good job anon
stop panicking moron, worst case scenario is you DON'T get $100,000 woOOoOoo spooky
>please respond I'm scared as fuck. Am I cooked or does this actually work? Anything I should learn before even starting or should I just give up and not show up? Anyone pull this off?
nigger go to bed
stop ruminating
the less you overthink, the better chances you have, trust me

62130816
>>62130783
>Can I just use Claude or Copilot to carry me?
don't do this
>I have never done any shipping of actual code ever. never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews. i just grinded python and leetcode 24/7.
just wing it
if you've done leetcode & had exposure to that shit you can do the rest, they're not gonna be doing anything high pressure
stay in the present moment & pay attention to what they're doing, don't get up in your own head about this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62131267
>>62130783
Bro I've been in IT and project delivery for 15 years and landed a startup job and I feel like im fucked but i think everyone at this stage is just larping anyway people just talk and talk "clean code, beautiful code, were agile" its boring its like people think coding is some art form and hold themselves so highly but really they are just wagies like everyone else and nobody cares as long as functionally its right the customer dont actually care what the code looks like

And retros are just group therapy with one guy swinging his dick like hes God but is really just a narcissist and scared to fail.

62131608
>>62130783
Based bro Indiamaxxing

62131675
>>62130783
>data analysis
Literally just use shitty AIs and mass horde as much of your income as possible and prepare to be caught.

62131683
>>62130783
Congratulations, 4 weeks is more than enough time to fake it better

62131688
>>62130783
Good news are you if you got in, the inertia will keep you for half year at least. If you keep proactively learning, people will notice you're fake but let it slide because you're providing value.

I had serious struggle rooting parasites like you, 5000-head company.

62131696
>>62130795
this. Op could have a 30 year career with nothing but confidence. Many such cases. That's why things are fucked, the boomers in charge never knew what the fuck they were doing either.

>your reckoning might never come you retarded piece of shit

62131714
>>62130783

I had a dream like this where I landed some big job that I wasn't qualified for, then when I show up to work they take me in front of a fucking nuclear reactor and tell me to fix it and when I can't they have security escort me out while everyone watches.

So good luck and I hope you do better!

62131724
>>62130783
>mid-level SWE job
>>62130783
>never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews.

ITfag here (cybersecurity). It you were in cybersecurity you could get away with this and learn git and how to use github and VS Studio Code and shit and just lean on your other expertise while you use ChatGPT to learn the CI/CD pipeline and tools.

But as a MID LEVEL SOFTWARE ENGINEER you are completely fucked and there is no way you are gonna pull this off but put me in the screencap when you come back and tell us how you embarrassed yourself and your chink/gook/jeet coworkers reported you to Chad Kinsington your team lead and he had HR Stacy fire you.

62131729
>>62130783
Troon out immediatly. Update your goybook and instacuck status and upload photos of you trooning out and dancing in gay bars. They can't fire you unless they do it because of transphobic reasons. They'll offer you severance for 2 years.

62131767
>>62130783
Don’t get emotional about your work

62131802
>>62130783
>Flat-out lied on my resume
>made a fake resume, somehow snagged six-figure mid-level SWE job
Congrats on becoming the typical H1B jeet. Now you just keep jeeting the company hy faking confidence and googling all the answers.
>Am I cooked or does this actually work?
Look around your office and see how many jeets you have. None of them do real work. Everyone of them learned just enough to scrape by. You're one of them now, the jeet class.
No shame in that when this is a market where you need to jeet or get jeeted.

62131813
>>62130783
based if you're white but either way who cares? what are they gonna do, fire you?
that takes a while.
Then you apply to the next job and use a burner cell to give yourself a good reference

62131815
>>62130783
The entire industry is just a bunch of lies, nothing's based on merit. Just kill yourself, bitch.

62131820
>>62130924
Are you really this helpless? You deserve to be unemployed.
>how to?
>find defunct company in [industry]
>put defuct company on your resume as [title of interest] with xyz years of experience
>"yes I was the techlead for the backend development at Toys R Us, Radioshack, and Blockbuster before they went out of business"
Use your brain anon. No one can verify your experience if the big name company no longer exists. Look for recent bankruptcies in your industry.

>>62131696
>Op could have a 30 year career with nothing but confidence
/thread
All jobs are fake anyways.

62131826
>>62130783
Sounds fake because they didnt check your degree.

62131836
>>62130783
>Flat-out lied on my resume
good. thats what EVERYONE does. its part of the game. rest is up to you. fake it until you make it.

62131845
>>62131802
>describes my resume
damn AI is getting good these days

62131848
>>62130783
Most jobs are actually pretty easy

62131852
>>62130783
Just wing it, ask your juniors how you do things in this company. Just be curious, keep learning and keep grinding. First 3 months will be the hardest. You can practice for free contributing to open-source software.

62132120
>>62130783
Keep faking it until you make it. But don't just sit there, use this opportunity to learn.

At worst they'll see right through you and let you go before probation but you make some good money anyways and learn a few things and get humbled. But you'll see how the business is organized how they operation, what tools they use, etc. this is valuable knowledge on its own, even if its just for personal use.

At best you find out the job is easier than it seems and that you can quickly get the ropes and start learning on the job,
BUT put the effort to learn.
You will get humbled, and you'll probably fuck up, but if you make it past probation, then its the companies fault for hiring you in the first place. Just don't do anything stupid and take responsibility for anything critical. This is an Opportunity. Seize it.

62132148
being a nice person with a positive attitude is arguably more important than technical competence. technical problems generally are solved and you can find the solution either thinking about it or with AI. not being a jerk is much harder.

62132167
>>62132148
This post encapsulates every thing I wanted to say. Social skills are the best thing anyone can have in life. I work as a programmer and I’ve completely checked out of my job, I mostly use Gemini to do data science, I give it the first 10 lines of each file and tell it to write the code exactly as I need it. The less prompting you do, the better the code quality. If you take too many prompts AI will start forgetting things and will leave out critical functions.

62132188
>>62130783
just stack cash, appear productive and do the bare minimum to get by
simple as

62132753
ITT: niggas think software developers have fake email girl jobs like everyone else.

62132790
>>62130783
This is literally exactly what I’m trying to do. Econ degree and about 2 years of work experience. Just want to scam my way into as high a position as possible. Lie on my resume, on everything. Any advice besides grind leetcode and python? Also, any other decent paying jobs adjacent to swe but not precisely swe? Did your fake resume have fake websites/references attached? Or do you just hope they don’t look things up. Salutations, nigga

62133049
>>62130919
Best adivce ITT. It's don't see how he could pull this off without something like this. Personalized tutoring from someone who have done this job in the past. He better have the money to pay.

62133363
>>62130783
... Isn't this what background checks are for?

62135404
>>62130783
First off stop freaking out. The worst they can do is let you go and put you back to where you are now.
Depending on the size of the company it may take a long time for them to notice you dont know what you are doing. Maybe even indefinitely.
If i were you i would start trying to learn the shit NOW, like right now. Just to get enough of an understanding to carry on conversations about work related topics. If you actually learn the shit, even better, but you need to be able to talk like you know it at a minimum. If you miss some super common acronym or abbreviation it will throw up a red flag
Outsourcing to Claude and ChatGPT is a great idea, definitely do that if you dont know what you are doing. However, you should supplement that with outsourcing to coworkers when possible. Be friendly with your coworkers and ask them to look over your work, or even write code for you if you think you can pull it off.
Lastly, try to move up the ladder as soon as possible. It sounds counter intuitive but the higher up you go in an organization, the more abstract your job duties become and the more you gain the ability to delegate tasks to other people if you dont know how to do it yourself.
Relax, you got this. People have done a lot more than you are doing now with a lot less resources available to do it with. My current boss’s boss is a completely incompetent moron, he just bullshitted his way there and he did it without the option of using AI. Good luck

62135542
>>62130783
If this company is so retarded that they didn't ask a single larp-busting question during a six figures job interview, then I legitimately believe you're good to go. Use your first few months to figure as much as you can.

62136113
>>62130783
At least you'll actually learn and gain hands on experience. Will be helpful for the next time you lie.

item.title: Investment forecasting: 2028 US Elections — 19 replies — 22.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investment forecasting: 2028 US Elections

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-04-20 18:41 UTC

OP

Current forecasting suggests that Gavin Newsom will run for president in 2028 and, given the current state of the Republican Party, that he will likely win. Running with that assumption, what would be the best investments to make now beforehand?

I’m very bullish on carbon credit markets.

Early replies (first 3)

62134469
>>62134463
I plan to vote Democrat for the first time in my life in November. Enough is enough.

62134471
how to profit from carbon credit markets? i looked into ctx for investing and/or trading but they need a high payment to onboard and the price of the credits is not really going up.

62134487
>>62134471
Compliance credits. KRBN ETF holds California, EU, RGGI, and UK carbon allowance futures.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134494
>>62134463
>skip ad

62134496
>>62134463
It depends I could see people not voting for him just because of how much of a shithole California is in. I hear there's homeless people everywhere and everything in expensive asf

62134497
>>62134463
Everyone hates Californians and Newsom has too many skeletons in his closet

62134529
>>62134463
AOC is the next president if Trump doesn't rig a third term. The best investment is to have a secondary passport. Preferably to a 3rd world shithole with high food production and clean water.

62134537
>>62134529
USA already rejected a white and brown woman, it’s over xister.

62134697
I'm thinking of buying a bush block, 12 acres of land full of trees and a creek running through it. Hopefully having land with trees will give me some benefits when carbon credits come in.

62134751
>>62134469
Ye 2028
Also, nice digits

62134826
>>62134463
>Read more about the current reality we live in >>533433939
Pharisees Jews vs Zionist Jews. Newsom is faction A, a Pharisees loyalist. He likely won’t win because of a Zionist Jew President in power now closing the gaps. Presidential elections are tightly controlled and won in less than 5% margins.

62134832
>>62134826
>>>>/pol/533433939

62134843
>>62134463
>what would be the best investments to make now beforehand?
5.56

62134975
Really love what the guy did for California, I'm sure he has many fans nationwide!

62135188
>>62134463
He will not be the nominee, he is a white man.

62135965
>>62134463
i feel like there is no dick he wouldnt suck, so probably everything. if we get stagflation/crabbing for the of trumps term it will be ready to pump

62135992
>>62134463
>Getting into prediction markets this far out
Did you ever hear the tragedy of anonbidenbull?

62136035
>>62134463
Commiecrats will likely win next election and thats a bad thing.
Higher taxes, stupid bicycle lanes, and open borders for 3rd world browns.
I'm planning on taking profit before they can enact tax hikes, put it all in crypto, and go travel for the next 4 years.

62136068
California treats regular fucking people, working regular fucking jobs, driving their regular fucking cars to and from work like the Federal government treats Smokers.
Gas is subject to a sin tax. The democratic supermajority considers anyone too poor to own a single family home, with a garage and it's own charging infrastructure, unworthy of operating a motor vehicle.

This is how democrats operate in principal across all economic regulation when they have total control over the legislature. They eviscerate the working middle class because that demographic is too white. They do everything in their power to inflate the K-shaped economy, eat the middle so the Elites and the Poor benefit.

item.title: AAVE will go back up right — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AAVE will go back up right

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-20 19:25 UTC

OP

AAVE will go back up right

Early replies (first 3)

62136190
Fuck no. Surprised it hasn’t been delisted on Robinhood already.

62136197
It is only too late, and after a year~ of back in the wageforce after leaving in 2021 that I realized if I had just been waging the whole time and only buying 15%+ dips on the stock market/crypto, DCAing into the bear, I would be up 3.4 million dollars instead of barely 100k and forced to work to put more into my 401k.

62136199
>>62136059
>alpha will go back up right
>yfi will go back up right
>rune will go back up right
>AAVE will go back up right
>[whatever exploited shitcoin] will go back up right
Anon, I...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62136214
it was finally recovering the day before this happened....

item.title: >new version of Brave called Origin where people pay $60 to remove BAT from the browser — 10 replies — 10.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>new version of Brave called Origin where people pay $60 to remove BAT from the browser

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-20 18:32 UTC

OP

lmao thank god i sold this shit

Early replies (first 3)

62133733
>>62133602
You're joking right? I've been holding 100k BAT since May 2017.

62133734
>>62133602
can we sue brendan eich for false advertisement and stealing our hard earned money for his bullshit shitcoin?

62134088
>>62133602
wait what, are you joking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134105
Holy shit, glad I got rid of this shit back in 2019 and never looked back.

62134245
>>62133733
>>62133733
>>62134088
>>62134105
>What is Brave Origin?
Brave Origin is a paid* version of the browser for users who don’t need all the features that support Brave as a business, but still want the privacy that only Brave offers. Origin users will continue to benefit from our industry-leading privacy, adblock, and speed (via Shields), as well as regular software updates, Chromium patches, and security and privacy improvements. Origin will remove** most other features from the browser.
https://support.brave.app/hc/en-us/articles/38561489788173-What-is-Brave-Origin
Its not for the sole purpose of removing BAT.

62134316
>>62134245
>Its not for the sole purpose of removing BAT.
>Look, we know we have a lot of absolutely shit features that absolutely nobody wants. Rather than basing our business around selling features that people DO want, we'll base it around charging to get the trash removed.
I'm not sure your clarification helped in any single way.

62134683
>>62133734
this fuck brave fuck bat

62135525
>>62134245
>>62134316
So Brave Origin will be Brave without BAT. You can frame it however you want, but that's a matter of fact. Origin will be the Brave browser without BAT. That's a sucker punch for BAT holders. It does feel like there's a developing case for misrepresentation or light fraud at this point against Brave for what they have done with BAT

62135576
>>62133602
It's obvious. You get BAT for free, Brave doesn't want it, so you pay to not have BAT.

>>62135525
Then sue them you weenie. Instead of whining about suing them for years.

62136038
that last thot that TP posted looked straight up like AI

item.title: ITT post your bear signals — 6 replies — 7.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT post your bear signals

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-20 18:40 UTC

OP

ITT post your bear signals

Early replies (first 3)

62131328
>>62131320
I am in crypto because I LOVE banks! Gluglugluglug banks! BANKS ARE THE BEST!

62133326
>>62131320
We're just posting bull news now and calling it bear. Anything is anything on this board

62133334
>>62131320
Just sold all my SCHD

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134490
>>62133334
Thx for cheapies retard

62136058
>>62131320
Crazy Schwab nearly went under a few years back when those other banks went under and now they are doing to fuck with crypto?

62136064
>>62134490

item.title: Living with your parents — 52 replies — 52.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Living with your parents

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-04-20 18:31 UTC

OP

Is this the ultimate /biz/ hack? I'm investing 50+% of my GROSS income

Early replies (first 3)

62133220
>>62133182
In most the world it's normal.
In America it was not normal since boomers could bug house with one month of work but the standard they put out is fastly eroding as housing is increasingly becoming unattainable with no relief in sight

62133227
>>62133220
not everyone's job/career is within driving distance/train/whatever of their parents house.

people move for work.

62133257
>>62133220
I could easily afford rent (good job relative to living in flyover country) but just seems better to invest that instead
>>62133227
That's fair but I'd think it would actually be easier in cities than it is for me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62133806
>>62133719
I have $324k. I've looked at houses but I always get outbid.

62133809
>>62133772
This.
Today I had a fight with my dad after he threatened to drive drunk (again). I even had to restrain him.
It's financially better (duh, of course) but it destroys you slowly.

62133827
>>62133182
My parents kicked me out when I was 19 but I’ve since forgave them. Today I have children of my own and I wouldn’t dare to do that to them. This topic comes up a lot with other parents my age and we all come to the same conclusion. The world is vastly different than it was in the 1990s and we have to do our this topic comes up a lot with other parents my age best to set them up right. In some ways Generation X had it easy.

62133828
>>62133827
>we have to do our best.

Sorry typo!

62133836
>>62133809
Your dad sounds based

No jk, he sounds like my dad, last time I brought my friend over and he totally freaked the fuck out over nothing and started yelling at me

Like bro I’m 29 years old, I’m just gonna go ahead and never talk to you again if you’re gonna be a fag

62133840
>>62133806
give em nominal monthly payment otherwise they will resent.

62133857
>>62133182
I make 10k a month and pay $500 for rent living with a coworker

62133876
>>62133836
I know you're joking, but there's nothing based about an alcoholic dad that is ruining other people's lives

Alcohol is really the ultimate goyslop. Poison for the soul.

62133879
>>62133182
If I wasn't living with my parents for years, I couldn't afford to buy a house in 2020 and would have been priced out.

62133938
>>62133857
How?

62133951
Stunted growth doesn't pay off in the long run..

62133961
>>62133876
My dad is also an alcoholic faggot

Millions of dollars networth and sits around hungover all the time in a filthy house

Fuck him

62134000
>>62133772
Same, of course as soon as I moved to a different city my dad starts blowing up my phone. Nah, nigga. You're getting 2 phone calls a year. I did everything I was asked for 20 years, got a decent degree on time, got a solid job at 22, and you still give me this shit. Just because I graduated during covid and had most of my plans scrapped for something that wasn't my fault. What a fucking faggot, I hate people like this

62134011
>>62133182
That's how immigrants move up IN AMERICA.

62134029
>>62133182
Depends. I'm 29 and still live with my parents; I just put a house under contract. If you're mortgaging a house it's going towards your equity so it's not like you're throwing it down a black hole. And some houses in my area have increased 100% within the past year. The house I'm buying last sold in 1998 for 35k and I offered 235k for it. The housing market is fucking stupid. So yeah, I wouldn't have made it this far (70th percentile net worth for my age) if I didn't bum it at my parents for long as I did and instead became a rentoid right after college.

On the other hand your social life get's knee capped HORRIBLEY living with your parents past the age of 22. I think I missed out on a lot. Inviting girls over to try and fuck them or inviting your bros over to have some beers and talk shit is out of the question. You can't do that with your parents in the background. Or idk maybe it's normal for thirdies to do it >>62133220. But it'd be beyond embarrassing for me.

Honestly, fuck boomers for making housing so unaffordable where the average joe can't move out by late teens/early 20s. Just another example of the elderly acting as vampire against the youth

62134300
>>62133182
>Is this the ultimate /biz/ hack?
Yes, but normies will mock you for it.
>>62133951
>Stunted growth doesn't pay off in the long run..
There's an argument to be made for this, but women are all chasing the top % of men; if that's not you, then you'll be living somewhere in a more expensive situation, continuing to watch women all chase the top % of men. If you're a pretty average guy and mildly spergy, then it's worth optimizing to make money.

62134440
>>62133182
It's the most OP strat, even if you're flipping burgers, you can retire in 20 years. Boomers don't even realize that it is possible.

62134447
>>62133182
Yes I live with my parents because it doesn't make sense to do otherwise for me right now. They're fine with it since they lived with their parents for a long time and I sometimes borrow them money when they need it so they don't have to pay interests to banks. In the mean time I pay almost nothing in terms of living costs and they'll remember me better than my sister when it comes to the will.

62134451
>>62133182
>>62133772
There's a reason why rent is so expensive. Imagine having to answer where/why you're going out at night, or being asked to do the dishes/take the trash out at 30 years old.

62134788
>>62134451
>To answer where/why you're going out at night
Set boundaries with your parents
>Or being asked to do the dishes/take the trash out at 30 years old.
You still need to do these things after moving out. Rinsing a plate and putting it in the dishwasher is no biggie.

62134816
>>62134788
>Set boundaries with your parents
It's still patronizing to be asked. They're not trying to be nosey, they're just curious, but it feels like you're still a child.
>You still need to do these things after moving out. Rinsing a plate and putting it in the dishwasher is no biggie.
Yes, but it's different when its your own stuff, and on your own terms. There are convenient times when I prever to do those things, like taking the trash out while on the way to the store, for example. My parents always wanted me to do what they asked immediately, even if I was about to go to bed or in the middle of eating.

62134905
>>62133182
Works nice for me as well. When I was 22, my mom signed the house on me under the condition she can stay. She buys groceries, cooks for me, cleans the house while I pay utilities and do basic maintenance. Now I am 26yo and thanks to this I am able to save half of my paycheck and do some investements. In few years I'll have enough to rebuild the house and live worry free. Would I spent those 4 years renting an apartment I'd have no money, no property and no future. What else can I say except let the renters deal with the real world?

62135090
>>62133182
>moved out for a few years after college
>moved back in to moms basement 10 years ago
>$175k/yr wfh job (didn't make decent money until the last few years)
>everyone who knows thinks I'm a loser but they would think that regardless of where I live
Honestly it's pretty comfy. She's old and widowed so I handle all the yardwork etc. We watch movies together.

62135104
>>62135090
>175k a year wfh
That's a lie.

62135247
>>62133182
Yes, but only if you sell your car and live at home.

62135480
I work from home in a LCOL apartment and still can invest 50%+ my gross income while not dealing with family bullshit AND getting daily pussy from my NEET gf that stays over while cooking and cleaning for me while I play vidya. Balance is key.

62135498
>>62135480
And then everyone clapped

62135945
>>62135104
I have a bunch of experience in a niche part of engineering that our company is heavily reliant on. The lead engineer quit and the company is having a hard time hiring new talent in this area so they've been bumping salaries significantly to keep the rest of us around. I know it won't last forever but I'm raking in the money while I can

62135975
>>62133182
yeah if you have decent parents live at home go at uni at the same place live at home and invest everything
yeah you will be set already
most important part: dont have retard parents (if you mess this up prepare to be set back decades)

62136036
Yes. Do it for as long as possible.

>>62133227
Skill issue.

item.title: >AAVE has hit 100% utilization on 3B+ dollars in stablecoins >literally zero threads about it Are you fuckheads too dumb… — 16 replies — 25.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>AAVE has hit 100% utilization on 3B+ dollars in stablecoins >literally zero threads about it Are you fuckheads too dumb…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-04-20 18:53 UTC

OP

>AAVE has hit 100% utilization on 3B+ dollars in stablecoins
>literally zero threads about it
Are you fuckheads too dumb to understand how bad this is? This deserves at least one "NUCLEAR HAPPENING" thread.

Early replies (first 3)

62135426
>>62135396
Explain it to me like I'm 5

62135435
>>62135396
>future of finance
it's over for crypto

62135449
>>62135396
>Future of finance was just to be a middle man for money market funds on stables

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62135466
> be in defi
> put usdc on aave at 3% apy
> "safe yield. lowest-risk play."
> can't withdraw because kelpdao got drained
> because kelpdao used layerzero
> because layerzero trusted 3 rpc servers
> because north korea ddos'd 3 rpc servers
> layerzero: "the protocol worked as intended"

62135467
>>62135426
Almost no stablecoins available for DEFI loans (people putting up their USDT/USDC etc for yield which is loaned to other people). This causes a spike in the APR (currently around 15%, average is about 3%). This will probably self-correct as people jump in for the higher yield, increasing supply.

62135479
Never trust a lending platform where they offer higher rates than short term treasuries

62135485
>>62135467
Will this increase all stake token yields?

62135502
>>62135485
Actually, seems I missed that there was some fuckup with bad debt. I asked Grok for a tl;dr:

TL;DR: Aave is in a major liquidity crisis and bad debt scare right now (April 19-20, 2026) triggered by a $292M KelpDAO rsETH bridge exploit.
What happened (super short)

Attacker drained rsETH (restaked ETH from KelpDAO) via a bridge exploit.
They dumped the now-unbacked rsETH onto Aave as collateral and borrowed ~$196-236M in WETH.
Result: Aave now sitting on ~$177-200M+ in bad debt (uncollectible loans).

Immediate fallout

Aave froze rsETH markets on V3 + V4 (and WETH in affected chains: Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, etc.) out of caution. Protocol itself was not hacked.
Panic withdrawals: $5-8B+ TVL evaporated in ~24h (one of the biggest single-day outflows ever).
WETH utilization hit 100% on Ethereum mainnet suppliers can’t easily withdraw. Other pools (USDT/USDC) also near full. Lenders are stuck in some cases.
AAVE token crashed ~20% to ~$90. Whales dumped millions.

Current status (as of Apr 20)

Aave says rsETH on Ethereum is still fully backed per their analysis, but frozen anyway while they assess fixes.
Team is isolating risk, no new borrowing against rsETH, actively working on resolutions.
V4 (just launched) and the rest of the protocol are still running, but confidence took a big hit.

This is the hottest topic in DeFi right now — classic “collateral suddenly becomes worthless cascade” risk that lending protocols always warn about. Situation is stabilizing but still tense; watch official @aave updates.

62135514
>>62135396
True and bump.

62135530
Had to Uniswap my shit because I couldn't withdraw, even Uniswap started to fail, future of finance guys.

62135565
should have used chainlink

62135573
>>62135396
I'm sure they'll figure it out. AAVE is the #1 defi platform lol. Some rich fags will loan them money with a lot of points and everything will be fine.

62135609
>>62135573
>too big to fail

62135955
>>62135573
Nigger those very same rich guys are the ones who pulled out immediately when they found out. Also the foundation agreed to a $25 million payout in exchange stakers get the protocol revenues. They volunteered to give up $150 million worth of revenue that they generated up until now. They know what’s up. Aave is gonna implode soon.

62136070
curve will turn aave into a powerhouse
and by curve I mean the Llamas

these low tier risk assessments are over
no more jeets

62136105
>>62135396
>WAAAA NUCLEAR HAPPENING
Do people still come here for happenings? This board is beyond dead.
eviscerated
bogged beyond all recognition.
You can find generals from ONE FUCKING MONTH ago and your usual LINK bagholder threads from 1 week ago. If it wasn't for /smg/ this place would average single digit posts per day. Anyways I'll see you at page 10 in about a week.

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 74 replies — 17.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-04-20 15:21 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121787
>>62121749
Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS

Who still owns the OS space?

Exactly

62121817
>>62121787
>Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS
Great that you point that out. That's an apples to oranges comparison. Linux isn't meant for the average user. It's meant for programmers in a lot of ways. We're also not talking about OS. Are we?

>>62121769
>Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
True, and no company survives in it's original form. Everyone adapts to the time. And the time is shifting towards a Web3 cloud.

>Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.
True, and I'm not saying that. What I'm saying is that Web3 will, over time, take a large share away from it, due to it being cheaper and having more uptime. Again, centralized servers CAN NOT compete with decentralized ones on uptime and cost. And those two factors are literally the only two important factors when it comes to a cloud provider. It has to be cheap and have the largest possible uptime.

>Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?
The thing is that Amazon will just use Web3 clouds. I doubt they'll build their own because they'd have to spent a lot of money to re-invent the wheel. DOT is already ready in a lot of ways. This year JAM hits and that'll transform it into a TRUE Web3 cloud capable of handling the load of AWS. Look, I'm not saying that DOT will IMMEDIATELY kill everyone. It'll be a slow transition and more and more companies will be switching. You know the 20/80 rule? It takes as much time to go from 0% to 20% than it does from 20% to 80%. This was true for smart phones, the internet, cars, ... So, if it took 10 years to go from 0% users to 20% of the population using something then it'll take that same amount of time to go from 20% to 80%. TO BE CONTINUED

62121830
>>62121769
Copied from Google AI

As of early 2026, over 650 million to nearly 1 billion people worldwide are estimated to own or use cryptocurrency. Growth is driven by high adoption in regions like Asia and Africa, with about 6.8% of the global population holding digital assets. While ownership is high, active monthly users are estimated lower, around 30–60 million, indicating a large population of passive holders.

Projected Growth: The number of users is expected to reach 993 million by 2026, with user penetration expected to reach 12.63% of the population, according to 1.
Regional Leaders: Asia dominates with 43% of global users, followed by North America (17%) and Europe (15%).
Top Adoption Countries: India, Nigeria, and Indonesia show high adoption rates, often driven by local currency instability, according to 4 and 3.
User Demographics: Approximately 60% of crypto users are under the age of 34, with a majority being male.

Active on-chain usage continues to rise as mobile wallet installations grow, with over 1.1 billion installations tracked as of early 2026.


We're almost at the 20% mark. BTC was created 17 years ago. So, we'll be at the 20% mark by like 2028 to 2030. So like 19 to 21 years. So, by the year 2050 80% of the world population will be using Web3.

62121862
I'm not sure if you guys are looking at the chart but we're really close to breaking out from the bottom
Look at picrel >>62118020 and >>62118038

62121888
>>62121769
Will it have enough marketing?
No worries my brother. I have made a marketing contract with Jeny Smith (https://thothub.mx/videos/1505023/jeny-smith-polka-dot-pantyhose/). She will market DOT for us. We are set my Mumbai brother

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121922
>>62121862
I'm not sure if you guys noticed. It's an adam and eve pattern on the 4h chart. Look at picrel

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62135378
>>62135362

>>62135378

62135411

62135414
>>62135362

62135415
>>62135362

62135416
>>62135362

62135551
>>62135362
I pretend to work for 90% of the time and then go into an aderall + adrenaline fueled panic productivity frenzy the day before a meeting to get enough done that it only seems I pretend to work 20% of the time.

62135629
>>62135362

clean hustling
neet gibs

62135635
>>62135629

62135641
>>62135362

62135659
>>62135635

you drive around at the end of the month and get free shit, from people moving/evicted/spring cleaning.

62135865

62135900
>>62135362

62135917
>>62135900

no cap: increase your total fee by 3% with each accurate posting of your face in your profile. bitches lap that shit up

62135928
>>62135362
I sell my sol to the wage bros, I only make $35/hr 40 hours a week and $190 in per diem and housing 365 days a year. I did a good amount of overtime for 1.5 pay last year though

62132093

62132441

seriously? You literally can just look at a picture of this guy and know he is drooling fucking retard. Of course anyone that still listens to him is a drooling retard. Why do you care?

62132469

62132566

62132588

62132620

62132746

62132780

62132819

62132859

item.title: >Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA >USSR collapses >Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA >Japan… — 24 replies — 8.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA >USSR collapses >Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA >Japan…

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-04-20 16:41 UTC

OP

>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>USSR collapses
>Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>Japan's economy completely tanks and they still haven't recovered
>EU overtakes USA for a short while, but starts to stagnate
>Chinese economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>China starts to stagnate while USA continues its steady growth
Is competing with America just a death wish economically? Why can't anyone win against the USA?

Early replies (first 3)

62134540
>>62134386
>Why can't anyone win against the USA?
Inevitably you end up with the US being your biggest trade partner, if you economically start eclipsing them it's ironically bad for business.

And that includes the USSR.

62134577
>>62134386
>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA
LOL that never happened. The USSR was a train wreck from the very start. Stalin managed to force industrialization on what had been an agrarian peasant society, but their factories were complete garbage and their productivity was always shit.

>Japan
>China
Both of them engaged in bubble financing and beggar-thy-neighbor mercantilism to try to pwn the U.S. Both of them ran out of money before they could wreck enough U.S. industries to take over.

>EU stagnates
They did it to themselves with all their political policies, ranging from "green energy" to "let's shit on white males and drive them out of society" to "hey we don't have enough workers so let's import illiterate stupid monkeys".

62134579
>>62134386
Literally nothing I own was made in America

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134700
Fox on the business board.

62134702
>>62134386
A: The Federal Reserve Banking Cartel.

62134711
>>62134700
Averi mating press.

62134718
>>62134700
what an oddly familiar looking vixen

62134723
>>62134577
Correct. They never teach you this in the USA but 200,000 Americans went to the Soviet Union along with hundreds of thousands of Westerners to help build it. They believed in socialism.

Other Western specialists like factory engineers and such were hired out by American companies to build Soviet factories.

Russia without non-Russian tech and specialists is an impossible shithole.
Even post 1990 who built the Russian oil and gas infrastructure?
German engineers, companies, and specialists.

62134785
>>62134579
So? Let the rest of the world make stuff for slave wages. Nevermind you cant get a factory job that pays enough for the average person to buy a home and raise a family, GDP number go up.

62134813
Suburbanmaxxing workchad innovators. No one can beat our chaotic soup here.

62134974
>>62134813
Crushing property taxes are unuronically one of the chief drivers of American wealth chasing. One cannot simply chill if he's staring down a 5 figure tax bill every year just to live in his house.

62135059
>>62134974

No, we just give people enough taste of ownership, of desire, to maximize ambition. Your own property, your own vehicle, things to take care of, stuff to dream about, now go chase the bag. This is rare internationally. Most people live in high density slums, commie blocks, apartments, as renters. There is no upside, it's just day to day subsistence.

62135163
>>62134577
>"hey we don't have enough workers so let's import illiterate stupid monkeys".
this is funny because this is exactly how the US won economically, just with the difference that you guys imported a ton of mexicans, asians and indians, while the EU went with turks, middle easterners and africans.

The reality is that your imported migrants were just more hardworking people

62135201
>>62134386
US was incredibly strong during these periods. That is not the case anymore.

62135273
>>62135201
America is growing faster than China and has been for the past few years, what are you talking about?

62135304
>>62135273
What are you on about? Chinese growth is greater than that of the US.

62135336
>>62135304
?

62135406
>>62135336
?

62135468
>>62134723
The Soviet Union's government-run farms were so bad, they had to buy wheat from Canada to avoid mass starvation (except for the people they wanted to starve). Guess how they paid for the Canadian wheat? Gold.

China had the same problem with the Great Leap Forward. But they just let people starve. The greatest famine in human history.

62135495
>>62135406
18.2 to 18.7 trillion isn't exactly a 5% growth

62135556
>>62134386

62135571
>>62135556
nobody in this thread is saying china is going to collapse or become bankrupt, but they are stagnating and the concept of taking america's spot as the #1 global economic power is now only a dream

62135617
>>62135059
>commie blocks
>renters
It's funny how Americans don't comprehend that home ownership without a mortgage is like 80% across the former Soviet region.
It may be an ugly commie block building but we own it and the apartment interior is usually nice.

62135640
>>62135495
You are right, it isn't, but nobody mentioned any of these two numbers so I don't know why you had the need to point that out.

item.title: Something is coming soon — 27 replies — 6.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Something is coming soon

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-04-20 15:20 UTC

OP

If you’re wondering where the recent FUD is coming from it’s because we’re only months away from the release of the CyberCode 2.0 testnet. As an insider, you have no idea what the importance of CbC 2.0 is going to be for LINK when the alpha releases around the end of November (give or take a few months) so I will spoonfeed you out of kindness of heart:

CyberCode 2.0 secures the ledger cryptographically to encode transactions at 20x of the legacy infrastructure. It achieves this by taking blockchain transactions to the BbC-2026 layer and parsing them through the node network incentivized by super linear sequencing (SLSeq) which Sergey said is very close to the finish line. This is all dynamically incentivized through semi-permeable staking (SPS) allowing LINK token holders to benefit for verifying data both on the main chain and the Blockchain Byzantine Consensus 2026 layer. No other project is even close to doing this in the oracle space rn. It’s not even close!

The obvious benefits of super fast transaction speeds (obSFTS) also brings exponential economies of scale. What this means is if farmer has 50 Kg pig for sale, he can put the beast to market on the blockchain to verify the weight in a trust less system faster than global SWIFT system. Soon any business who wants to stay relevant will have to buy LINK to have access to the CyberCode 2.0 feature. A truly unique proposition! Those who don’t use this system (powered by LINK) will get left behind. So get your ducks in a row. Just keep buying and NEVER sell. We are all in this together LINKbloodbros.

Scammer motherfuckers want you to think that banks and other organizations are not currently fulling their bags with cheap LINK as we speak. DON’T NOT LET THEM WIN! If you hold 50 or more LINK right now, you already made it. Immediately when we breach the $10 resistance it’ll be too late for you. You have been warned. So go buy many LINK for you dream to come true.

Early replies (first 3)

62125665
>>62125647
DR;NS

62125781
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless???

62125919
FUDDIES, ROLL OUT

62125940

62125959

62125996

better wait a few more years for the PoC testnet beta to pass its initial audit round

62126215
Holy fuck a good opportunity to buy a house with gf has come up and I might seriously have to sell my measly stack before it fuckin moons, I can't believe this shit Ive been holding for fucking years and years

>hide thread

62126836

62126992

62127837
>>62125647

62127858
>>62125647

This CbC2.0 bullshit sounds like that BBC(A) shit that old jewish bitch at CLL came up with. Unfortunately the kind of jews at CLL are not the ones that make you money, but rather the scamming and subverting kind. We linkies will never makeit unless Sirgay unironically stops shoving bigmacs in every hole of his constantly (not happening).

62127911
the best part of this pasta is the farmer and pig

62130613
>>62125647

Hahaha very funny

62130614

62130617

62131808

62131940

62131995

item.title: How much BTC you holding right now? — 91 replies — 5.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC you holding right now?

Open thread • Replies: 91 • Updated: 2026-04-20 04:10 UTC

OP

How much BTC do you currently have in your wallet, and what’s your target amount to hold before it reaches $1M?

>I mean, if you don’t even have at least 1 BTC and you’re in crypto, it still feels pointless.

Early replies (first 3)

62114988
>>62114930
1.03

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62123856
>>62115773
Not enough

62123911
>>62121496
Just sell it today bobo

62124101
>>62114930
I hold 0.84 btc at Kraken

62124449
>>62123590
You have a point here tho. The use cases are still early, so skepticism makes sense. What’s interesting is the idea of extending utility while keeping Bitcoin unchanged and kept in self custody.

62124459
>>62116911
>ETH is cool but why LINK?

62124487
2.3 BTC and 36 ETH.

62124590
>>62115225
Giga Chad, I kneel

62124643
>>62117007
His choice lol

62125521
>>62117185
$30k? Let's see if you're right.

62125560
1.4 BTC

It doesn't feel like enough. I'm 36 now, and my dream of living a wealthy and comfy life before the age of 40 is basically done for. Is it even possible to have a fun life at 50+? I really lament not getting into BTC back in 2014. It's not like I didn't know about it back then.

62126507
Is BTC even worth having anymore? Seems to have had pretty shit returns since the covid boom.

62126533
>>62114930
https://timesofmalta.com/article/elusive-bitcoin-founder-adam-back-lives-malta.1126648
What are the financial implications of this?

62126561
>>62123814
What risk are you talking about here? Also, it’s better now that there are returns instead of just holding with no yield. This setup is way better overall.

62126610
>>62117395
but why did you sell?

62126617
>>62117832
buy the bottom? but at which price?

62127919
>>62124487
More!!!

62128353
>>62120218
YOU CAN DO IT BOI

62128654
>>62125560
Don't worry about it. Keep going and have fun. YOLO.

62130077
>>62122141

62130099
0.3

62131443
>>62130099
Just 0.3? Make it whole bitcoin

62131447
>>62126507
since the COVID boom? It was just $8k during covid

62131531
>>62114930
The new paradigm is no more peaks.

62131569
>>62114930
Zero and zero

62132043
1.72 bitcoins
*sigh*
I keep stacking, maybe one day, I'll have 2 whole bitcoins.

62132222
around 10, i might end up keeping them forever as a backup plan. Looking at my taxes my average accumulation price was 29k and sell price was 109k Tidy little 70k each. I'll buy back in when the dump is over and it settles between 20-30k. for supposedly being a crypto board you all sure seem to struggle with basic pattern recognition, this is exactly what happens every time, dump 75% from the peak and then the next bull run starts.

62132243
>>62115773
I remember that 21 used to be the suistack. Everyone's goal was to accumulate AT LEAST 21 btc.

62132565
>>62131531
Why?

62133350
>>62131447

62133953
>>62131569
That's bad.
